kw.name: Check a value is less than a number
kw.doc: check if a value is less than a number :return: return_status
kw.shortdoc: check if a value is less than a number :return: return_status

kw.name: Convert MLP to Text
kw.doc:
Convert the MLP Log to text file
:argument - ``mlp_file`` : the path of mlp ivi or mlp safe (required) - ``txt_file`` : the path of text file (required) - ``start_bit`` : the bit of start reading
:return - seek_bit : the bit of end reading
Examples: | `Convert MLP to Text` | mlp_file=${Log Path}\${SUITE NAME}.mlp_ivi | txt_file=${Log Path}\${SUITE NAME}.txt | | `Convert MLP to Text` | mlp_file=${Log Path}\${SUITE NAME}.mlp_ivi | txt_file=${Log Path}\${SUITE NAME}.txt | start_bit=29130 |
kw.shortdoc: Convert the MLP Log to text file

kw.name: Find CAN Message in ASC File
Find String in Log File
kw.doc:
Find string in saved log ( ``logFilePath``)
:argument - ``logFilePath`` : path of log file - ``findString`` : Keyword you want to find. Multiple keywords can be received through the delimiter (||) ex) keyword_A||keyword_B - ``exclude`` : Keyword you want to ignore. When if find ``findString``, If you want ignore keyword in same line. use this. Multiple keywords can be received through the delimiter (&&) - ``start_line`` : starting line reading a log. type is int
:returns - ``result`` : If find return True, else return False. type is boolean. - ``search_index`` : If find, return searching line . else return end of file line.
Examples: | ${result} | ${CPU_INDEX} | `Find String in Log File` | find_Keyword | ignore_keyword | 100 | | ${result} | ${CPU_INDEX} | `Find String in Log File` | find_Keyword_1||find_Keyword_2 | ignore_keyword_1&&ignore_keyword_2 | start_line=80 |

kw.name: Find String in Log File And Return Output
kw.doc:
Find string in saved log ( ``logFilePath``)
:argument - ``logFilePath`` : path of log file - ``findString`` : Keyword you want to find. Multiple keywords can be received through the delimiter (||) ex) keyword_A||keyword_B - ``exclude`` : Keyword you want to ignore. When if find ``findString``, If you want ignore keyword in same line. use this. Multiple keywords can be received through the delimiter (&&) - ``start_line`` : starting line reading a log. type is int
:returns - ``result`` : If find return True, else return False. type is boolean. - ``search_index`` : If find, return searching line . else return end of file line.
Examples: | ${result} | ${CPU_INDEX} | `Find String in Log File` | find_Keyword | ignore_keyword | 100 | | ${result} | ${CPU_INDEX} | `Find String in Log File` | find_Keyword_1||find_Keyword_2 | ignore_keyword_1&&ignore_keyword_2 | start_line=80 |
